issue_summary: Changes to iterative linear solvers in package o.a.c.m.linear PreconditionedIterativeLinearSolver is now provided with the *inverse* of the preconditioner as a RealLinearOperator, rather than the preconditioner as an InvertibleRealLinearOperator InvertibleRealLinearOperator has become superfluous and is removed various javadoc fixes
javadoc_before: /** * This abstract class defines preconditioned iterative solvers. When A is * ill-conditioned, instead of solving system A · x = b directly, it is * preferable to solve M-1 · A · x = M-1 * · b, where M approximates in some way A, while remaining comparatively * easier to invert. M (not M-1!) is called the * preconditionner. * * @version $Id$ * @since 3.0 */
javadoc_after:
/** *

* This abstract class defines preconditioned iterative solvers. When A is * ill-conditioned, instead of solving system A · x = b directly, it is * preferable to solve M-1 · A · x = M-1 * · b, where M approximates in some way A, while remaining comparatively * easier to invert. M (not M-1!) is called the * preconditionner. *

*

* Concrete implementations of this abstract class must be provided with * M-1, the inverse of the preconditioner, as a * {@link RealLinearOperator}. *

* * @version $Id$ * @since 3.0 */
